product_updates.title: Bluesky leans into AI with Attie, an app for building custom feeds
product_updates.content: Bluesky’s new app Attie uses AI to help people build custom feeds the open social networking protocol atproto.

product_updates.title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
product_updates.content: While there’s been plenty of debate about AI sycophancy, a new study by Stanford computer scientists attempts to measure how harmful that tendency might be.

product_updates.title: Elon Musk’s last co-founder reportedly leaves xAI
product_updates.content: All but two of Musk's 11 xAI co-founders departed before this week.

product_updates.title: Anthropic’s Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
product_updates.content: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell TechCrunch that Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year.

technical_breakthroughs.title: A woman’s uterus has been kept alive outside the body for the first time
technical_breakthroughs.content: “Think of this as a human body,” says Javier González. In front of me is essentially a metal box on wheels. Standing at around a meter in height, it reminds me of a stainless-steel counter in a restaurant kitchen. It is covered in flexible plastic tubing—which act as veins and arteries—connecting…
